channel.title: Agent Engineering on Agent Memory — AI Memory Systems Explained
channel.description: Recent content in Agent Engineering on Agent Memory — AI Memory Systems Explained

item.title: LLM Memory Engineering: Crafting Persistent Recall for AI Agents
item.description: LLM memory engineering is the practice of designing and implementing memory architectures for Large Language Models (LLMs) to enable persistent, contextual recall beyond their inherent context window limitations. This discipline focuses on building sophisticated memory systems that allow AI agents to store, retrieve, and use information over extended interactions, unlocking truly intelligent behavior. This capability is essential for AI agents to perform complex tasks and provide consistent, personalized user experiences over time.
